title: Grocery Chain Margin Decline
background: A regional grocery chain has seen profit margins decline despite stable traffic across its stores.
candidate_prompt: How would you evaluate the situation and recommend what the organization should do next?
good_answer_includes: Breaks profit into revenue and cost drivers
Tests price, volume, mix, and retention changes
Examines labor, supplier, occupancy, and operating costs
Prioritizes actions by financial impact
great_answer_includes: Separates market trends from company-specific issues
Segments performance by customer, location, and product
Quantifies the biggest drivers before recommending actions
Balances margin improvement with customer experience
interviewer_notes: A strong candidate should use a clear profitability structure, isolate the most important drivers, and connect the recommendation to business impact.

title: Telehealth Expansion into Canada
background: A US telehealth company is considering launching in Canada after seeing strong domestic growth.

good_answer_includes: Defines target customer and unmet need
Assesses market demand and competitive alternatives
Considers pricing, channel, and launch timing
Recommends a launch plan with success metrics
great_answer_includes: Identifies adoption barriers and early user segments
Designs pilot, feedback, and iteration approach
Balances brand, margin, and operational complexity
Specifies go/no-go criteria
interviewer_notes: A strong candidate should use a clear product launch structure, isolate the most important drivers, and connect the recommendation to business impact.

title: Restaurant Delivery Service GTM
background: A restaurant technology company is launching a delivery management tool for independent restaurants.
candidate_prompt: How would you evaluate the situation and recommend what the organization should do next?
good_answer_includes: Defines target segments and buyer personas
Chooses channels, messaging, and sales motion
Considers pricing, enablement, and launch sequencing
Recommends a GTM plan
great_answer_includes: Aligns GTM motion with deal size and buying process
Identifies early adopter segments and proof points
Considers partner and direct channel tradeoffs
Defines pipeline, conversion, and retention metrics
interviewer_notes: A strong candidate should use a clear go-to-market structure, isolate the most important drivers, and connect the recommendation to business impact.

title: Cloud Backup Market Size
background: A software company wants to estimate the market for cloud backup services among small businesses.
candidate_prompt: How would you evaluate the situation and recommend what the organization should do next?
good_answer_includes: Defines the market clearly and avoids double counting
Uses a logical top-down or bottom-up structure
States assumptions transparently
Calculates a reasonable estimate
great_answer_includes: Validates assumptions with sanity checks
Segments the market where useful
Separates users, units, frequency, and price
Connects market size to business attractiveness
interviewer_notes: A strong candidate should use a clear market sizing structure, isolate the most important drivers, and connect the recommendation to business impact.

title: Grocery Delivery Price War
background: A grocery delivery company is facing aggressive discounts from a larger competitor.
candidate_prompt: How would you evaluate the situation and recommend what the organization should do next?
good_answer_includes: Identifies the competitor's offer and target customers
Assesses impact on price, volume, and retention
Evaluates response options beyond matching price
Recommends a focused response
great_answer_includes: Segments vulnerable customers and defensible strengths
Considers likely competitor reactions
Balances short-term defense with long-term positioning
Defines metrics to monitor response effectiveness
interviewer_notes: A strong candidate should use a clear competitive response structure, isolate the most important drivers, and connect the recommendation to business impact.

title: School District Budget Gap
background: A school district faces a budget gap and wants to reduce costs while preserving classroom quality.
candidate_prompt: How would you evaluate the situation and recommend what the organization should do next?
good_answer_includes: Breaks costs into major categories
Separates fixed and variable costs
Identifies savings opportunities with minimal customer impact
Prioritizes savings by size and feasibility
great_answer_includes: Avoids cuts that harm revenue or service quality
Benchmarks costs against comparable organizations
Includes one-time versus recurring savings
Creates a realistic implementation and risk plan
interviewer_notes: A strong candidate should use a clear cost reduction structure, isolate the most important drivers, and connect the recommendation to business impact.

title: Retail Saas Strategy Decision
background: A retail organization is facing a SaaS strategy decision and wants to identify the best path forward.
candidate_prompt: How would you evaluate the situation and recommend what the organization should do next?
good_answer_includes: Breaks performance into acquisition, activation, retention, and expansion
Assesses pricing, packaging, and customer segments
Identifies product and sales levers
Recommends a SaaS growth plan
great_answer_includes: Uses metrics such as churn, CAC, LTV, and NRR
Segments customers by size, usage, and profitability
Balances growth with unit economics
Defines experiments and leading indicators
interviewer_notes: A strong candidate should use a clear saas strategy structure, isolate the most important drivers, and connect the recommendation to business impact.

title: Technology Cybersecurity Improvement Plan
background: A technology organization is facing a cybersecurity improvement plan and wants to identify the best path forward.
candidate_prompt: How would you evaluate the situation and recommend what the organization should do next?
good_answer_includes: Identifies key assets, threats, and vulnerabilities
Assesses current controls and incident readiness
Prioritizes risks by likelihood and impact
Recommends security investments
great_answer_includes: Balances risk reduction with business operations
Considers employee behavior and third-party exposure
Defines governance, monitoring, and response capabilities
Prioritizes quick wins and longer-term resilience
interviewer_notes: A strong candidate should use a clear cybersecurity structure, isolate the most important drivers, and connect the recommendation to business impact.

title: Technology Data and Analytics Roadmap
background: A technology organization is facing a data and analytics roadmap and wants to identify the best path forward.
candidate_prompt: How would you evaluate the situation and recommend what the organization should do next?
good_answer_includes: Clarifies business questions analytics should answer
Assesses data sources, quality, and ownership
Identifies needed dashboards, models, or insights
Recommends an analytics roadmap
great_answer_includes: Connects analytics to decisions and measurable outcomes
Addresses data governance and adoption barriers
Prioritizes use cases by value and feasibility
Defines operating model and accountability
interviewer_notes: A strong candidate should use a clear data and analytics structure, isolate the most important drivers, and connect the recommendation to business impact.

title: Technology Ai and Automation Opportunity
background: A technology organization is facing a AI and automation opportunity and wants to identify the best path forward.
candidate_prompt: How would you evaluate the situation and recommend what the organization should do next?
good_answer_includes: Identifies processes suitable for automation
Assesses data availability, risk, and human oversight needs
Estimates productivity or quality impact
Recommends use cases to pilot
great_answer_includes: Prioritizes use cases by value and feasibility
Considers model accuracy, bias, and governance
Defines human-in-the-loop controls
Plans measurement before scaling
interviewer_notes: A strong candidate should use a clear ai and automation structure, isolate the most important drivers, and connect the recommendation to business impact.
